title: fix(core): widen TrailingAssistantGuard to block all trailing assistant messages for Claude 4.6

similarOrDuplicatePRs.title: fix(core): prevent trailing assistant message with structured output
similarOrDuplicatePRs.relationship: predecessor — this PR extends the original guard added in #12835

relatedIssues.title: TrailingAssistantGuard too narrow — Claude 4.6 rejects all trailing assistant messages, not just structured output

summary: The TrailingAssistantGuard processor (added in #12835) only appended a user message when structured output was active. Claude 4.6 removed assistant message prefilling entirely, so any trailing assistant message — thread resumption, agent handoffs, tool-call rounds — causes a 400 error. This PR removes the early return on willUseResponseFormat so the guard fires for all trailing assistant messages. The injected text is contextual: 'Generate the structured response.' for structured output, 'Continue.' otherwise. Changes span 3 files: a changeset, 185 lines of new tests, and a small logic change in trailing-assistant-guard.ts.
maintainerNotes: Well-scoped community fix for a real bug (#13969). The code change is minimal and correct: the willUseResponseFormat check is moved from a gate that skips the guard entirely to a conditional that selects the appropriate continuation text. The 185-line test file is thorough, covering structured output, plain trailing assistant, user-last no-op, and isMaybeClaude46 detection. CodeRabbit flagged minor issues (test type strictness, changeset wording) which the author addressed. CI tests are still pending (stuck at 'Setup actions with secrets' — likely needs org secrets for fork PRs). Vercel checks fail due to authorization (expected for external contributors). No merge conflicts. This is a logical extension of #12835 and addresses a valid reported issue.
